questions: What is the module structure for the new CLI library commands?
How does library item resolution work across multiple reference formats?
What commands and flags does the CLI need for library comments?
What needs to be added to SKILL.md for agent discoverability?

answers.answer: New src/library/ module with 6 files: index.ts (router dispatching to list, show, comments), list.ts, show.ts, comments.ts (nested router dispatching to comments-list, comments-post), comments-list.ts, comments-post.ts. Plus src/lib/resolve-library-item.ts utility. Follows the exact pattern of src/comments/ (router -> cmd* functions).

Three strategies with priority: (1) cuid — if input starts with 'c' and is 25 chars, use as item ID directly; (2) ticket short ID — if matches /^[A-Z]+-\d+$/ (e.g., RSH-439), resolve via API to find latest library item for that ticket; (3) title match — fallback to case-insensitive title substring match on all items.

4 commands: hlx library list (no flags), hlx library show <ref> (optional --full), hlx library comments list <ref> (optional --section <slug>), hlx library comments post <ref> --section <slug> --rating <value> [message]. Rating accepts thumbs-up/love/thumbs-down with shorthands up/down/love. Section flag auto-slugifies text input containing spaces or uppercase.

A new ## Library section in skill-content/SKILL.md documenting all 4 commands with flag descriptions, ref resolution, and rating values. This is critical because agents read SKILL.md to discover available CLI capabilities.

statement: Complete CLI feature gap: no library module exists. Implementation plan covers 9 ordered steps: resolve-library-item utility, module router, 4 command files, main dispatcher integration, and SKILL.md update. Follows established src/comments/ module pattern exactly. All commands non-interactive, use standard flag parsing via src/lib/flags.ts.
